refnamediv.refpurpose: Fit an ellipse to two-dimensional point coordinates.

listitem.para: N-by-2 matrix of at least five [x y] coordinates.

simplelist.member: Axes: full major-axis and minor-axis lengths.
Angle: major-axis orientation in radians, measured in the input coordinate system.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Estimates an ellipse from the centroid and covariance of the input points. The method is compact and deterministic, but it is not a robust outlier-resistant estimator.
Contour coordinates returned by IPCV use an upper-left image origin. Convert sampled and fitted coordinates with rect2cart before drawing them with Scilab plotting functions.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Source reference: OpenCV contour-features tutorial: fitting an ellipse.

tr.td: Function introduced with covariance-based ellipse fitting.
